event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: The facade accepts only one existing accepted N static-description composition and one existing accepted X three-owner snapshot; it performs no provider call or ambient acquisition.
One explicit call projects accepted X fields through P, consumes N and P through Q, and borrows Q through R to produce the bounded observed document, removing caller-owned intermediate orchestration.

The implementation, runtime, and transport witnesses move unchanged into three separately inspectable artifacts beside the finished document without comparison, aggregation, or common-epoch inference.
Caller-selected P, Q, and R limits are delegated unchanged and failures identify the exact rejected stage without alternate acceptance policy.

The facade adds no parser, raw endpoint or wire claim, runtime activation, common owner epochs, freshness, authorization, endpoint semantics, discovery, clock, socket, network, device, dependency, feature, or Manifold authority.

document.commit_policy: Commit the accepted N/X-to-R local facade separately from parsers, protocol or endpoint wire behavior, runtime activation, devices, dependencies, features, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the evidence-preserving N/X-through-R composition and prohibit document-to-runtime or interoperability inference.
Describe the dependency-completing local document facade while retaining the three owner witnesses.
Review artifact-role and owner-evidence guidance; retain unchanged because the facade preserves existing owner artifacts and authority boundaries.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review Rusty LSL and Manifold routing; retain unchanged because no repository ownership, activation, or runtime route changes.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Provider selection or calls, retries, background work, clock, environment, hostname, interface, or network inspection, identity generation, address or port parsing, sockets, network reads or writes, reachability, authorization, or platform adapters.
Cross-owner identity equality, common epoch or revision, atomic acquisition, freshness, currentness, witness aggregation, or a new authority decision.

Modification of N, P, Q, R, X, O, or S acceptance policy; parsing; raw endpoint or wire shape; protocol or ecosystem interoperability; discovery; inlet; outlet; buffering; recovery; FFI; runtime activation; devices; packages; permissions; dependencies; features; or effects.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, authorization, audit, session, topology, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Consume one accepted LSLC-001N static-description composition and one accepted LSLC-001X three-owner snapshot; project the accepted snapshot fields through P, consume N and P through Q, project Q through R, and return the bounded observed document beside the unchanged three separately inspectable owner witnesses, without adding acquisition, parsing, wire, runtime, device, dependency, feature, or authority behavior.
outputs: A bounded facade that composes only accepted N and X state through the existing P, Q, and R contracts.
A finished observed document paired with the unchanged three-owner evidence object.
Stage-typed P, Q, and R delegated errors under explicit caller-selected limits.
Focused exact-document, separate-witness, stage-rejection, source-only, and ambient-surface validation.
